meta.title: RigPolice Embed live demo

meta.description: A published post with the RigPolice Tool block rendering a live CPS test. The widget loads from rigpolice.com in its own frame and auto-resizes.

steps.code:
4, 'post_title' => 'How Fast Can You Click? Try the CPS Test', 'post_status' => 'publish', 'post_type' => 'post', 'post_content' => '

Clicks per second, or CPS, is a simple way to measure how quickly you can tap your mouse in a set window of time. Gamers use it to compare mice, to warm up before a match, and to see whether a new grip or a lighter switch actually helps them click faster.

The test below is embedded straight into this post with the RigPolice Tool block. It loads in its own frame, resizes to fit the page, and runs entirely in your browser. Give it a try: click inside the frame to start the timer, then click as fast as you can until it stops.

Most people land somewhere between six and nine clicks per second on a normal click. If you want a higher score, techniques like jitter clicking and butterfly clicking can push the number up, though they take practice and are not allowed on every server.

You can drop this same block into any post or page, pick a different tool, and publish. Readers get a hands-on test right where they are reading, with no account and nothing tracking them.

' ) );
